Notice And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299774 The Fesko Family Legacy Trust, PTR-18-301520 The Helen Fesko Exempt Trust and PTR-18-301521 The Jack Fesko Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Duane Horning appeared by zoom for M. David Fesko (deceased Former Client).
Jon Vaught appeared by zoom for John Fesko.

Mr. Horning clarified that he is asking for him and Tracy Schimelfenig to be relieved and that the scope of the motion is for the entire law firm to be removed. The Motions to Be Relieved as Counsel (3) are under submission with the Court's intent to grant. The Court will write in Mr. Horning's Partners' name.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting; To Recover Property Wrongfully Taken And For Constructive Trust; Damages For Breach Of Trust; For Double Damages And Attor
Off Calendar May-14-2019 Continued To Aug-13-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299774 The Fesko Family Legacy Trust, PTR-18-301520 The Helen Fesko Exempt Trust and PTR-18-301521 The Jack Fesko Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Duane Horning appeared by telephone for David Fesko.
Jon Vaught appeared by telephone for John Fesko.

The Court noted that it saw the notice of the Bankruptcy Court and questions whether or not some of these matters could go forward pending the stay. Mr. Horning stated that at least two of the cases (the Helen Trust and the Jack Trust) are stayed because there are claims against David, that all three cases need to be tried together and that it is his understanding that the Bankruptcy Counsel will be filing a motion for relief from the stay within the next few weeks so that these matters could go forward. Mr. Vaught stated that the Bankruptcy Counsel indicated that he shouldn't file anything in these cases without a further order from the Bankruptcy Court regarding the stay. The Second Amended Petition to Determine Trustee of the Trust, etc., Petition of Ethel Merriman for Accounting and Damages for Breach of Trust, Petition to Invalidate Trust, etc., Petitions for An Accounting to Recover Property Wrongfully Taken, etc. (2), and Petitions for Accounting to Redress Breaches of Trust, etc. (2) are continued to August 13, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If the Bankruptcy Court hasn't given a total green light, the Parties are to file something as to what this Court is able to do.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting; To Recover Property Wrongfully Taken And For Constructive Trust; Damages For Breach Of Trust; For Double Damages And Attor
Off Calendar Oct-09-2018 Continued To Jan-08-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-16-299774 The Fesko Family Legacy Trust, PTR-18-301520 The Helen Fesko Trust and PTR-18-301521 The Jack Fesko Exempt Trust.

Appearances: Duane Horning appeared by telephone for and with David Fesko (present).
Michael Freedman for and with David Fesko (in the Jack Trust and the Helen Trust).
Jon Vaught for John Fesko.

Mr. Freedman stated that he and Mr. Vaught agreed to do private mediation, that he believes they have a tentative agreement as to who the Mediator will be and that he thinks mediation will be completed in mid December. Mr. Vaught stated that the San Diego trial concluded in favor of his Client and that there is an issue of Mr. Fesko having taken some assets out of the partnerships. Mr. Horning stated that he has not been part of the mediation discussions, but he has no objection to mediation. The Petition for an Accounting; to Recover Property Wrongfully Taken and for Constructive Trust, etc. filed by John Fesko on June 12, 2017 is off calendar. Mr. Vaught confirmed that a dismissal was filed as to said petition.
The Second Amended Petition to Determine Trustee of the Trust, etc., Petition of Ethel Merriman for Accounting and Damages for Breach of Trust, Petition to Invalidate Trust, etc., Petitions for An Accounting to Recover Property Wrongfully Taken, etc. (2), and Petitions for Accounting to Redress Breaches of Trust, etc. (2) are continued to January 8, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Parties are to file a joint status statement no later than December 31, 2018. If the matter is not resolved and needs to go to trial, the Parties are inform the Court as to what the issues are left, who the witnesses will be, how many exhibits they will have, the length of trial, etc.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Of Lack Of Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Jul-25-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

The motion to dismiss is denied. The Court clearly has subject matter jurisdiction over the subject petition (and David Fesko's responding petition). The Court also has personal jurisdiction over Ms. Merriman given that the alleged claims arise at least in part from her conduct in California. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling verbatim and show compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Petition For An Accounting; To Recover Property Wrongfully Taken And For Constructive Trust; Damages For Breach Of Trust; For Double Damages And Attor
Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Continued To Apr-24-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following cases were heard: PTR-18-301520 The Helen Fesko Trust and PTR-18-301521 The Jack Fesko Trust.

Appearances: Jon Vaught for Petitioner.
Matthew Matiasevich for and with David Fesko.
Ethel Merriman appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Counsel that it does not have an order establishing Jack Fesko's Trust. Mr. Matiasevich stated that he attempted to file petitions last week to compel John to act and 850 petitions, that the Clerk told him they needed to be filed in new trust case number and that he will work on the titles of the documents so that they can be filed in the same case number. Ms. Merriman stated that she has nothing to do with this and requested to be dismissed from the cases. The Court advised Ms. Merriman to seek the assistance from an Attorney and stated that this issue needs to be presented by way of a proper motion. Mr. Matiasevich stated that documents requests are complete and suggested the Legacy Trust follow these cases. Mr. Vaught informed the Court that they will be filing a joint status report today. Both Petitions for Accounting, etc. are continued to April 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing in the Legacy Trust (299774) on calendar for February 27, 2018 is also continued to April 24, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is to be filed by April 17, 2018.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
